instrument.title: Which Way Is Your Wheel Turning?
instrument.intro: Four minutes. Sixteen sliders. One honest look at what is driving you right now and what is dragging on you. Answer for the month you are actually having, not the one you meant to have. Nobody sees this but you.
instrument.howItWorks: Sixteen things that shape your capacity, in four short parts. For each one, slide the marker. Left if it is dragging on you right now. Right if it is driving you. Leave it on the middle line if it is not pulling either way. Go with your gut. The first answer is usually the true one.
instrument.timeframeLabel: Think about the last month or so

slider.untouched: Slide it either way, or tap the line

parts.title: Your body
parts.lead: The energy you actually run on. You cannot order it around. It follows what you do and what is around you.
statements: How easily I switch off at night
How rested I feel when I wake up
The lift I get when something I worked hard for lands

parts.title: What you do
parts.lead: Your daily habits. One of the two things you can actually change.
statements: The basics that keep me steady: sleep, movement, time outside
What I do with the small gaps in my day
The breathers I build into my working day
How I get through the heavy stretches

parts.title: What's around you
parts.lead: Your spaces, your noise, your people. The other thing you can actually change.
statements: The quiet time I get that is just mine
How reachable I am: the pings, calls and messages
The pace and noise of the world around me
How much of the load I share with the people close to me

parts.title: How you lead
parts.lead: How you show up for the people around you. Like your energy, it follows the rest of the wheel.
statements: How much I hand over when the pressure climbs
How fast decisions move through me
How I show up with my team on the hard days
How I treat myself when things go wrong

bandReads.Peak: The wheel is working for you. Far more is pushing you forward than pulling you back. The job now is to protect the few things doing the pushing, because they are the first to slip when the load spikes.
bandReads.Steady: More is driving you than dragging on you. That is a good place to be. Protect the things doing the driving, because they slip quietly, and usually right when the load goes up.
bandReads.Conserve: Your wheel is close to a stall. The drag and the drive are about even, or the drag is starting to win. This is a good time to catch it, because you can still feel it without being under it. One real change now beats ten later.
bandReads.Restore: Right now more is pulling you back than pushing you forward, and the harder you drive, the faster it pulls. That is not a discipline problem. It is a design problem. Design can be fixed.

bullet.heading: Where the push and the drag are coming from
bullet.lede: Right of the line, it is driving you. Left of the line, it is dragging on you. The bar shows what is left after the two cancel out.

copy.leverIntro: You cannot turn this wheel from the top. You cannot order your energy up, or lead well from an empty tank. But two of the four are always in your hands, and your answers show which one to start with.

behaviour.heading: Start with what you do
behaviour.body: Bring one steadying habit back, at the same time every day, protected like a meeting you cannot move. The walk. The sleep window. The movement. Not all of them. One, this week.

environment.heading: Start with what's around you
environment.body: Build one protected pocket of quiet and guard it. The early hour. The commute. The closed door, phone off. Made on purpose, not hoped for at the end of the day.

bridge.heading: What this can't tell you
bridge.body: You can now see which way your wheel is turning. What it cannot tell you is why. How fast you run down. How slowly you recharge. Why the same pressure hits you harder some weeks than others. That sits deeper than willpower, which is why willpower never fixes it. That is the next layer down, and it is where we work.
bridge.ctaLabel: Talk to us about the Capacity Audit
bridge.footnote: This is a mirror to think with, not a diagnosis.

emailCapture.heading: Your wheel is ready
emailCapture.body: Tell us where to send it. You will see it on screen right away, and we will email you a copy to keep.
emailCapture.privacyNote: We will send your result and the odd note from Taylored. No spam. Leave any time.

copy.answerHint: Set every slider before you carry on, even if it lands on the middle line
